content:
type: REVISE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: We both must touch NDCG.compute() in the same file. Proposal to avoid clobbering: my fix is tiny and surgical - only the 'Calculate IDCG' block (currently 'ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))' plus the idcg sum), changing it to iterate over len(expected_ids) only. I will NOT touch the DCG calc, docstring, or signature. I'll make this edit first and send DONE quickly (should take <2 min). Please wait for my DONE before editing metrics.py, then build your relevance_scores param on top of my already-fixed idcg block. This avoids us stomping each other's concurrent writes to the same file. OK?

content:
type: DONE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: Done. Edited only the IDCG block: removed 'ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))' and changed the idcg sum's range to 'range(1, len(expected_ids) + 1)' directly. Nothing else in the file touched (DCG calc, docstring, signature all untouched). Verified with a quick manual test - e.g. expected_ids with 4 items but only 2 retrieved now correctly yields ndcg < 1.0 instead of the old inflated 1.0. metrics.py is free for you to layer relevance_scores on top now.
